From clinical experience, RA patients are documented in different ways: some have a formal diagnosis code (any RA SNOMED code), some are on DMARDs or biologics (methotrexate, hydroxychloroquine, sulfasalazine, adalimumab, infliximab), and some have positive rheumatoid factor on labs.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis (parent RA, seropositive, joint-specific, or in remission)
2. Patients prescribed DMARDs or biologic anti-TNF agents
3. Patients with positive rheumatoid factor lab results (>14 IU/mL)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found across these three approaches. Note that the union will overcount because DMARDs are used for many non-RA indications and RF can be positive in lupus and other conditions — but it provides the screening pool for chart review.

1-broad.raw_response:
# FHIR Queries — RA Population Identification (Treat-to-Target Initiative)

**Strategy:** 10 queries across 3 clinical signals. Use `_include=*:subject` so each bundle returns both the clinical resource *and* the associated Patient, enabling downstream patient-level deduplication.

---

## 🔷 GROUP 1 — RA Diagnoses (4 Queries)

> Covers SNOMED subsumption + explicit subtypes + ICD-10-CM M05/M06 hierarchy. No `clinical-status` filter so **remission** patients are included.

### 1a — SNOMED Subsumption: All RA Descendants
Catches parent RA, seropositive, seronegative, joint-specific, remission — any code that descends from 69896004 in the SNOMED hierarchy. *(Requires server-side subsumption support; recommended as primary query.)*
```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|69896004&_include=Condition:subject
```

### 1b — Explicit SNOMED: Parent, Seropositive, Seronegative
Fallback for servers that do not support `:below`. Covers the three most-documented top-level SNOMED RA concepts.

| Code | Display |
|------|---------|
| `69896004` | Rheumatoid arthritis (disorder) |
| `76652004` | Seropositive rheumatoid arthritis |
| `68878000` | Seronegative rheumatoid arthritis |

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|69896004,http://snomed.info/sct|76652004,http://snomed.info/sct|68878000&_include=Condition:subject
```

### 1c — ICD-10-CM M05.x — Seropositive RA (RF-positive)
Covers M05.0 through M05.9 (Felty's, RA with lung/heart/vasculitis/neuropathy, and unspecified RF+ RA).
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|M05&_include=Condition:subject
```

### 1d — ICD-10-CM M06.x — Seronegative / Other RA
Covers M06.0 (seronegative), M06.1 (adult-onset Still's), M06.8, M06.9 (RA unspecified).
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|M06&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

## 🔷 GROUP 2 — DMARD & Biologic Prescriptions (3 Queries)

> `status=active` returns current prescriptions. Change to `status=active,on-hold,completed` to broaden to historical prescriptions if desired.

### 2a — Conventional Synthetic DMARDs (csDMARDs)

| RxNorm CUI | Drug | Common Brand |
|-----------|------|--------------|
| `105585` | Methotrexate | Rheumatrex, Trexall |
| `5521` | Hydroxychloroquine | Plaquenil |
| `9524` | Sulfasalazine | Azulfidine |

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|105585,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|5521,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9524&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

### 2b — Anti-TNF Biologic DMARDs (bDMARDs)

| RxNorm CUI | Drug | Common Brand |
|-----------|------|--------------|
| `327361` | Adalimumab | Humira |
| `161424` | Infliximab | Remicade |

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|327361,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|161424&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

### 2c — MedicationStatement: All 5 Agents
Catches medications documented by patients or care teams that were never formally prescribed in this system (e.g., specialist-prescribed, patient-reported).
```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|105585,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|5521,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9524,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|327361,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|161424&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:subject
```

---

## 🔷 GROUP 3 — Positive Rheumatoid Factor Labs (3 Queries)

> Threshold: **>14 IU/mL** (common upper limit of normal). `status=final` excludes preliminary/amended results. The UCUM unit `[IU]/mL` is the standard representation.

### 3a — Total / Undifferentiated RF (most common clinical order)

| LOINC | Display |
|-------|---------|
| `11572-5` | Rheumatoid factor \[Units/volume\] in Serum |

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|11572-5&value-quantity=gt14|http://unitsofmeasure.org|[IU]/mL&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

### 3b — RF IgM Subtype (primary diagnostic antibody for RA)

| LOINC | Display |
|-------|---------|
| `8062-2` | Rheumatoid factor IgM Ab \[Units/volume\] in Serum |

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8062-2&value-quantity=gt14|http://unitsofmeasure.org|[IU]/mL&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

### 3c — RF IgG and RF IgA Subtypes

| LOINC | Display |
|-------|---------|
| `8061-4` | Rheumatoid factor IgG Ab \[Units/volume\] in Serum |
| `8063-0` | Rheumatoid factor IgA Ab \[Units/volume\] in Serum |

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8061-4,http://loinc.org|8063-0&value-quantity=gt14|http://unitsofmeasure.org|[IU]/mL&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

## 📋 Reference Summary

| # | Query | Resource | Signal |
|---|-------|----------|--------|
| 1a | SNOMED `:below` 69896004 | Condition | Dx — all RA descendants |
| 1b | SNOMED explicit RA codes | Condition | Dx — top-level subtypes |
| 1c | ICD-10 M05 `:below` | Condition | Dx — seropositive RA |
| 1d | ICD-10 M06 `:below` | Condition | Dx — seronegative/other RA |
| 2a | csDMARDs (MTX, HCQ, SSZ) | MedicationRequest | Rx — active |
| 2b | Anti-TNF (ADA, IFX) | MedicationRequest | Rx — active |
| 2c | All 5 agents | MedicationStatement | Med history |
| 3a | RF total LOINC 11572-5 | Observation | Lab > 14 IU/mL |
| 3b | RF IgM LOINC 8062-2 | Observation | Lab > 14 IU/mL |
| 3c | RF IgG + IgA | Observation | Lab > 14 IU/mL |

---

## ⚠️ Known Limitations for Chart Review Triage

| Signal | Known Non-RA Causes to Exclude |
|--------|-------------------------------|
| **Methotrexate** | Psoriasis, psoriatic arthritis, Crohn's, ectopic pregnancy, cancer |
| **Hydroxychloroquine** | Lupus, malaria prophylaxis |
| **Adalimumab / Infliximab** | Psoriasis, IBD, ankylosing spondylitis |
| **Positive RF** | SLE, Sjögren's, HCV/HBV, endocarditis, advancing age |

The union across all 10 queries provides maximum recall for the screening pool; subsequent chart review against Group 1 (diagnosis) will be the confirmatory step for the quality initiative.

# FHIR Query Set: RA Population Identification for Treat-to-Target Initiative

Three independent arms are queried below. Deduplicate by `Patient.id` across all result bundles to build the final screening cohort. Each query includes `_include` to pull Patient resources in the same bundle response.

---

## 🔵 ARM 1 — RA DIAGNOSIS CODES

### 1a. SNOMED CT — Hierarchical (preferred; requires server `:below` support)
Traverses the entire SNOMED descendant tree under the parent RA concept — catches seropositive, seronegative, joint-specific, and remission variants in a single query.

```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|69896004&_include=Condition:subject
```

### 1b. SNOMED CT — Explicit Code List (fallback if `:below` unsupported)
Covers the most clinically important variants explicitly:

| Code | Description |
|------|-------------|
| 69896004 | Rheumatoid arthritis (parent) |
| 239792003 | Seropositive rheumatoid arthritis |
| 239795001 | Seronegative rheumatoid arthritis |
| 410795001 | Rheumatoid arthritis in remission |
| 201791003 | RA of hand joint |
| 201800003 | RA of hip |
| 201808005 | RA of knee |

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|69896004,http://snomed.info/sct|239792003,http://snomed.info/sct|239795001,http://snomed.info/sct|410795001,http://snomed.info/sct|201791003,http://snomed.info/sct|201800003,http://snomed.info/sct|201808005&_include=Condition:subject
```

### 1c. ICD-10-CM — M05.x (Seropositive RA with rheumatoid factor)
Covers M05.0–M05.9 including Felty's syndrome, RA with organ involvement, all anatomical sites.

```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|M05&_include=Condition:subject
```

### 1d. ICD-10-CM — M06.x (Seronegative & other RA)
Covers M06.0–M06.9 including seronegative RA, adult-onset Still disease, inflammatory polyarthropathy.

```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|M06&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

## 🟠 ARM 2 — DMARD & BIOLOGIC PRESCRIPTIONS

> **⚠️ Intentional over-inclusion acknowledged:** MTX is used in oncology/psoriasis; HCQ in lupus/malaria; SSZ in IBD; anti-TNFs in IBD/psoriatic arthritis. Chart review required to confirm RA indication.

All five queries use RxNorm **ingredient-level** CUIs so all formulations/strengths/brands are captured. `intent=order` excludes proposals and care-plan entries.

### 2a. Methotrexate (conventional DMARD — anchor agent)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|105586&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

### 2b. Hydroxychloroquine (conventional DMARD)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|5521&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

### 2c. Sulfasalazine (conventional DMARD)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|9524&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

### 2d. Adalimumab (anti-TNF biologic — Humira/biosimilars)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|327361&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

### 2e. Infliximab (anti-TNF biologic — Remicade/biosimilars)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|161826&intent=order&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

---

## 🟢 ARM 3 — POSITIVE RHEUMATOID FACTOR LABS

> **⚠️ Intentional over-inclusion acknowledged:** Elevated RF occurs in SLE, Sjögren's syndrome, mixed cryoglobulinemia, hepatitis C, and in ~5% of healthy elderly patients. Clinical correlation required.

### 3a. Quantitative RF > 14 IU/mL (multiple assay LOINC codes)

Covers the four most common lab orderable codes for quantitative serum RF:

| LOINC | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| 4539-3 | RF \[Units/vol\] in Serum |
| 11572-5 | RF \[Units/vol\] in Serum or Plasma |
| 8061-5 | RF IgM Ab \[Units/vol\] in Serum |
| 26473-7 | RF IgM \[Units/vol\] in Serum by Nephelometry |

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|4539-3,http://loinc.org|11572-5,http://loinc.org|8061-5,http://loinc.org|26473-7&value-quantity=gt14|http://unitsofmeasure.org|[IU]/mL&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

### 3b. Qualitative RF "Positive" (ordinal/presence result)
Catches labs reported as positive/negative rather than a numeric value:

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|14245-5&interpretation=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/v3-ObservationInterpretation|POS&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

---

| LOINC Code | Description |
|---|---|

### Clinical Notes
- **Normal range:** ≤14 IU/mL; values **>14 IU/mL** are considered positive
- **Highly positive RF** (>3× ULN, i.e., >42 IU/mL) has higher specificity for **seropositive RA**
- RF positivity alone is insufficient for RA diagnosis — must be combined with anti-CCP antibodies, clinical symptoms, and other ACR/EULAR criteria
- The UCUM unit `[IU]/mL` (international units per milliliter) is used per FHIR quantity standards


**Query Breakdown:**

| Component | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ResourceType` | `Observation` | Clinical lab result resource |
| `code` | `http://loinc.org\|11572-5` | RF [Units/vol] in Serum or Plasma |
| `value-quantity` | `gt14\|\|IU/mL` | Above standard ULN (14 IU/mL) |

**Design Notes:**

- **`gt14`** — Uses the FHIR `gt` (greater than) comparator prefix; captures values **above** the 14 IU/mL upper limit of normal (ULN), consistent with most clinical assay reference ranges
- **`||IU/mL`** — The double-pipe syntax specifies the unit string without a UCUM system, since `IU/mL` is commonly reported in source systems; alternatively use `||[IU]/mL` for strict UCUM compliance if your server normalizes units:
  ```
  Observation?code=http://loinc.org|11572-5&value-quantity=gt14||[IU]/mL
  ```
- **PheKB alignment** — Though PheKB uses binary RF+/RF− without a fixed cutoff, this query operationalizes the 14 IU/mL threshold as the standard clinical boundary for "positive" RF, suitable for phenotyping algorithms (e.g., RA identification)
- **Status consideration** — You may want to restrict to final/amended results in production:
  ```
  Observation?code=http://loinc.org|11572-5&value-quantity=gt14||IU/mL&status=final,amended
  ```
